data.title: Wingstop stock surged 12% on earnings beat despite revenue miss. Short interest at 17%, $175.69M sales, $1.3B global sales, 124 net new restaurants. Domestic same-store sales down 5.8%.
data.description: Wingstop Inc. saw a significant surge in its stock price, rising by over 12% on Wednesday, following the release of its earnings report. Despite a slight miss on quarterly revenue, the company outperformed profit expectations. The fourth-quarter saw Wingstop achieve adjusted earnings per share of $1, surpassing the anticipated 84 cents. Quarterly sales totaled $175.69 million, slightly below the expected $177.533 million. In the same period, system-wide sales grew by 9.3% from 2024 to reach $1.3 billion. Moreover, the company successfully opened 124 new restaurants, with a domestic restaurant AUV of $2.0 million. However, domestic same-store sales experienced a 5.8% decline compared to the previous year. Notably, digital orders contributed to 73.2% of total sales. Furthermore, short interest, already high at over 17% of the float, likely played a role in boosting buying pressure.

data.title: Crypto venture capital firm, Dragonfly Capital, raises $650M for fourth fund, despite market challenges.
data.description: Dragonfly Capital, a prominent crypto venture capital company, has recently finished raising funds for its fourth fund. The specialized venture capital fund, with a focus on blockchain technology, managed to raise a substantial $650 million. This achievement stands out as one of the largest fundraising efforts within the sector according to a recent Coindesk article. Haseeb Qureshi, the managing partner at Dragonfly, expressed mixed emotions about the success of the fund, acknowledging the challenging market conditions and prevailing fear in the industry. Despite the difficulties, Qureshi highlighted the firm's history of raising funds during bear markets, citing the success of Fund I in 2018. For additional details, the full article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Insulet Corp. upsurges with Q4 earnings beating $1.55 per share, sales of $783.8M (31.2% YoY), total Omnipod revenue $781.8M (33.5%).
data.description: Insulet Corporation's (NASDAQ: PODD) stock is experiencing an increase following the release of its fourth-quarter earnings report and 2026 guidance. The company exceeded expectations with fourth-quarter adjusted earnings of $1.55 per share, surpassing the consensus of $1.45. Sales for the quarter reached $783.8 million, marking a 31.2% increase year over year or 29% in constant currency (CC), exceeding the consensus of $768.69 million. Total Omnipod revenue amounted to $781.8 million, reflecting a 33.5% increase or 31.3% in constant currency. Additionally, Drug Delivery revenue was reported at $2.0 million. The company achieved a gross margin of 72.5%, up 40 basis points from the previous year. Operating income reached $146.3 million, accounting for 18.7% of revenue and marking a 40-basis point increase from the prior year. Adjusted operating income was at $146.2 million.

data.title: Northborne Partners advises FORTA's acquisition by The Heritage Group. Deal announced on Feb. 18, 2026. No financial terms disclosed.
data.description: Northborne Partners, a financial advisory firm based in Minneapolis, revealed on February 18, 2026, that it assisted FORTA, a company owned by Riverarch Equity Partners, in its acquisition by The Heritage Group. The specific financial details of the transaction were not disclosed.

data.title: Energy Vault secures $135.5M financing, posts strong Q4 2025 results. Expanding into AI infrastructure and Australia.
data.description: Energy Vault Holdings, Inc. has successfully concluded a financing deal, generating $135.5 million for the Company post initial purchaser commissions. The company, known for sustainable grid-scale energy storage and AI power infrastructure, has also shared key points from its Q4 2025 financial results. This includes advancements into AI infrastructure and expansions in Australia. Energy Vault's stock, listed as NRGV on NYSE, showcases its position as a global leader in the industry.

data.title: FTK finishes $13.6MM LIHTC renovation in Dallas, Texas.
data.description: FTK Construction Services, based in Allen, Texas, recently finished renovating a property located in Dallas. The completion of this project marks another successful endeavor for the company in the region. This accomplishment showcases FTK Construction Services' commitment to providing quality construction services.

data.title: Mission Cloud partners with Vantage for AI-enabled FinOps on AWS, enhancing cost management for enterprise clients.
data.description: In Vernon Hills, Illinois, on February 18, 2026, Mission Cloud, a subsidiary of CDW and a prominent U.S.-based Amazon Web Services (AWS) Premier Tier Services and ISV Accelerate Partner, revealed a fresh collaboration with Vantage. Vantage is a cutting-edge cloud cost management platform designed for enterprise use.

data.title: Inventor from Jackson, N.J. creates innovative toilet seat cover, named ALLE, to offer improved coverage and protection.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on February 18, 2026, an inventor from Jackson, N.J. shared plans for a new sanitary toilet seat cover called the ALLE. This invention aims to offer enhanced coverage and protection during bathroom use at home or while traveling. The inventor expressed the desire to address this issue by creating a product designed to meet this need, emphasizing the importance of cleanliness and comfort. The ALLE is set to introduce innovative features to improve the overall toilet seat cover experience, highlighting a shift towards improved hygiene practices.

data.title: Draper and NEMC partner to accelerate tech commercialization by offering chip design and packaging services to startups and small companies.
data.description: Two major entities, Draper and the Northeast Microelectronics Coalition (NEMC), have joined forces in Cambridge, Mass. and Boston as of February 18, 2026. In an effort to support startups and small companies within NEMC's membership, Draper will be offering advisory services and assistance to help de-risk and speed up the transition from lab to fabrication. This collaboration aims to provide crucial support to emerging companies, ultimately boosting innovation and growth in the microelectronics sector.

data.title: AI stocks are struggling as software sector slides. Ed Yardeni says AI is "skating on ice", warns of obsolescence. Investors moving to old economy sectors like energy and materials. Software stocks taking the first hit.
data.description: The anticipated rise of AI to drive the next phase of the bull market and revolutionize industries is now facing turbulence, with software stocks plummeting and investors returning to traditional sectors. Veteran strategist Ed Yardeni compared the situation to "speed skating on ice," highlighting the rapid change and disruption within the AI realm. The pace of evolution in AI hardware and large language models has escalated, causing unease among investors who are offloading companies deemed at risk of being replaced. Software stocks have taken a hit, signaling a shift away from tech leaders towards energy, materials, and other sectors in the tangible economy. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software ETF... (Remaining details can be found on Benzinga.com)
